name: ICT Trading Strategy Strategy
author: ICT

entry_conditions.rule: Identify the primary trend using market structure (higher highs/higher lows = uptrend; lower highs/lower lows = downtrend)
Wait for confirmed pattern breakout before entering — a pattern is only complete on actual breakout
Apply confirmation filters (intrabar, multiple closes, time, percentage) before acting on a breakout
For gap plays: wait for throwback after breakout gap — if throwback does not fill gap, place buy entry above high of gap bar (pivot)

exit_conditions.rule: For liquidity sweep longs: target next swing high (next buy-side liquidity pool)
For liquidity sweep shorts: target next swing low (next sell-side liquidity pool)
Set take-profit at structural highs (for longs) or structural lows (for shorts)
Calculate price target for double top/bottom: height from peak to trough projected from breakout point
For Head and Shoulders: target = distance from head to neckline, projected down from neckline break
For triangles: target = height of pattern added to (or subtracted from) breakout price
Exit or tighten stop when gap is violated after gap entry — trend assumption breaks down
Once in SMC trade, target the next structural high (long) or structural low (short)

risk_management.rule: Place stop loss below the order block for long trades (bullish setup)
Place stop loss above the order block for short trades (bearish setup)
Always leave a spread buffer when setting stop loss to mitigate slippage
Use stop-and-reverse orders on false breakouts: enter on breakout, place protective stop outside breakout bar, and entry stop at same level in opposite direction
Use risk-reward ratio when setting take profit — fixed ratios (e.g. 1:2, 1:3) are acceptable
Trading is a game of probability — never expect 100% win rate; cut losses and let winners run
Place protective stops based on: trend line / support-resistance level with filter, or percentage/points filter
Set stop loss for gap entries initially at gap low, then move below pivot low as trade develops

indicators.name: Relative Strength Index (RSI)
indicators.formula: RSI = 100 - 100/(1 + RS), where RS = avg up closes / avg down closes over period
indicators.how_to_use: Momentum oscillator measuring speed and change of price movements; overbought above 70, oversold below 30

indicators.name: Moving Averages
indicators.how_to_use: Average of closing prices over a period; used for trend direction and crossover signals
indicators.crossover_signal: When faster MA crosses above slower MA — bullish; below — bearish

indicators.name: Volume
indicators.how_to_use: Confirms breakouts — high volume on breakout = valid; low volume = suspect. High volume + large price gap near trend end = exhaustion gap

indicators.name: Fibonacci Retracement
indicators.how_to_use: Key levels: 23.6%, 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%, 78.6%. ICT uses 61.8%-78.6% for OTE. Traders place orders at these levels creating self-fulfilling liquidity zones

psychology: Trading always carries risk — losses are part of the game; success requires disciplined execution
Do not see patterns where there are none — human tendency to find patterns is dangerous
Do not stick with original price targets after conditions have changed
Patience is required — ICT/SMC requires waiting for specific market conditions before entry
Do not look backwards — focus on forward-looking analysis
SMC narrows trade opportunities by design — not every price move is tradeable
Greater Fool Theory: never buy at any price assuming someone will pay more — value matters

trend_identification.uptrend: Series of higher highs and higher lows
trend_identification.downtrend: Series of lower highs and lower lows
trend_identification.break_of_structure_BOS: Price sets a new high or low while breaking through former ones — signals trend continuation
trend_identification.change_of_character_CHOCH: Sudden shift in volatility, volume, or price action suggesting trend weakness and possible reversal
trend_identification.market_structure_shift_MSS: In an uptrend: a lower low forms. In a downtrend: a higher high forms. Usually follows displacement

support_and_resistance.support: Price level where demand is strong enough to prevent further decline; buyers step in as price cheapens
support_and_resistance.resistance: Price level where selling pressure prevents further advance; sellers increase and buyers decrease
support_and_resistance.role_reversal: A broken resistance level becomes new support, and vice versa

head_and_shoulders_top.description: Three peaks with center (head) higher than two outer peaks (shoulders)
head_and_shoulders_top.signal: Bearish reversal at end of uptrend
head_and_shoulders_top.entry: Break below neckline (line connecting the two troughs between peaks)
head_and_shoulders_top.target: Distance from head to neckline, projected downward from neckline
head_and_shoulders_top.reliability: One of the lowest failure rates of all patterns

inverse_head_and_shoulders.description: Three troughs with center lower than outer troughs
inverse_head_and_shoulders.signal: Bullish reversal at end of downtrend
inverse_head_and_shoulders.target: Distance from neckline to head, projected upward

double_top.description: Two successive peaks at roughly the same price level separated by a trough
double_top.target: Height from peak to trough, subtracted from breakout price

double_bottom.description: Two successive troughs at roughly the same price level separated by a peak
double_bottom.target: Height from trough to peak, added to breakout price

triple_top.description: Three distinct peaks at roughly the same price level
triple_top.entry: Break below the lowest trough or trendline connecting troughs
triple_top.target: Height from highest peak to lowest trough, subtracted from lowest trough

triple_bottom.description: Three distinct troughs at roughly the same price level
triple_bottom.signal: Bullish reversal — best after sustained decline
triple_bottom.entry: Break above highest peak or trendline connecting peaks
triple_bottom.target: Height from peak to trough, added to highest peak

rounding_bottom.description: Gradual U-shaped bottom over long period; also called saucer bottom
rounding_bottom.signal: Long-term bullish reversal; best on weekly charts
rounding_bottom.entry: Breakout above both lips of the cup
rounding_bottom.note: Performance ranks about average for bottom patterns

flag.description: Short consolidation (rectangle/parallelogram) sloping opposite to prior sharp move
flag.entry: Breakout in direction of prior trend
flag.target: Equal to height of the flagpole

pennant.description: Short consolidation in a small triangle shape after a sharp move
pennant.target: Equal to height of the flagpole

cup_and_handle.description: Rounded bottom (not V-shaped) with two lips, followed by a handle (flag-like consolidation)
cup_and_handle.entry: Breakout above both lips from the handle
cup_and_handle.target: Height of right cup lip to bottom of cup, added to breakout price

symmetrical.description: Downward upper trendline + upward lower trendline converging
symmetrical.breakout: Can break either direction; best performance is upward
symmetrical.note: Many false breakouts — always confirm breakout
symmetrical.target: Height of pattern added/subtracted from breakout price

ascending.description: Horizontal upper trendline + upward sloping lower trendline
ascending.target: Height of pattern added/subtracted from breakout price

descending.description: Downward sloping upper trendline + horizontal lower trendline
descending.note: Above-average performance on upside break; retracements common
descending.target: Height of pattern added/subtracted from breakout price

wedge_rising.description: Two trendlines heading same direction upward; price must touch each at least 5 times

wedge_falling.description: Two trendlines heading same direction downward
wedge_falling.breakout: Usually upward — bullish signal after panic/decline

rectangle.description: Trading range with horizontal support and resistance; often has many false breakouts
rectangle.shortfall: A shortfall (price failing to reach opposite side of range) often indicates eventual breakout direction
rectangle.target: Height of range added (upside) or subtracted (downside) from breakout

types.breakaway_gap: Occurs when price breaks out of a congestion area; requires high enthusiasm/volume
types.runaway_gap: Also called measuring gap; occurs mid-trend as traders chase moves; represents panic buying/selling
types.exhaustion_gap: Near end of trend; identified by high volume and large price difference from prior close; signals trend ending

gap_pivot_trade.setup: Wait for throwback after gap; if throwback stops without filling gap, that is the pivot low
gap_pivot_trade.entry: Buy entry above high of the gap bar
gap_pivot_trade.stop: Initially at gap low; move below pivot low

gaps.principle: Gaps generally profitable on breakouts from patterns, trends, support/resistance
